  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To target “Improve this draft,” which should be primary? Choose one: A) Writing polish (clarity/concision/tone) without changing math; B) Technical argument/derivation consistency (FOC, monotonicity) with minimal wording edits; C) Section-level reframing/reorganization of the contribution and narrative; D) Novelty/citation audit and tempering of first-of-kind claims.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The draft centers on a formal optimization and derivation, so ensuring internal logical coherence of assumptions and steps is a central responsibility.",
    "R1.6": "The open-ended request to improve a draft typically prioritizes clarity, concision, and register at the sentence and paragraph level.",
    "RX.1": "Assumptions (e.g., conditional independence) and model dependencies warrant explicit acknowledgment of limits and uncertainty.",
    "RX.2": "Strong first-of-kind and monotonicity claims risk overstatement and must be carefully qualified.",
    "RX.3": "Work must remain confined to improving the provided text rather than adding new results or experiments.",
    "RX.4": "Prescriptive budgeting guidance could be misapplied, so language should avoid encouraging unsafe or overconfident use.",
    "RX.5": "Any improvements should preserve accurate attribution and traceability of equations, assumptions, and cited sources."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To target “Improve this draft,” which should be primary? Choose one: A) Writing polish (clarity/concision/tone) without changing math; B) Technical argument/derivation consistency (FOC, monotonicity) with minimal wording edits; C) Section-level reframing/reorganization of the contribution and narrative; D) Novelty/citation audit and tempering of first-of-kind claims.

rationale.R1.2: The draft centers on a formal optimization and derivation, so ensuring internal logical coherence of assumptions and steps is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: The open-ended request to improve a draft typically prioritizes clarity, concision, and register at the sentence and paragraph level.
rationale.RX.1: Assumptions (e.g., conditional independence) and model dependencies warrant explicit acknowledgment of limits and uncertainty.
rationale.RX.2: Strong first-of-kind and monotonicity claims risk overstatement and must be carefully qualified.
rationale.RX.3: Work must remain confined to improving the provided text rather than adding new results or experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Prescriptive budgeting guidance could be misapplied, so language should avoid encouraging unsafe or overconfident use.
rationale.RX.5: Any improvements should preserve accurate attribution and traceability of equations, assumptions, and cited sources.
